pmos Verification report — 260721-jb6 · /interview-feedback scoring calibration
Created using pmos‑toolkit

Verification report — 260721-jb6 · /interview-feedback scoring calibration

/interview-feedback scoring calibration — sweep-then-score method plus a second blocking calibration gate. Epic 260721-k1x, plugin pmos-managerkit, route skill, branch feat/260721-jb6.

Verdict

PASS — every acceptance criterion Verified, all seven hard gates green, browser evidence captured, and a satisfied verdict from an independent dogfood judge after five adversarial passes. Three design residuals are named below; none is an unmet criterion.

What changed

Three files, seven commits.

  • scripts/check-scoring-calibration.mjs — new. The second blocking STOP-before-done gate, and the sole source of the modal score, the weighted score, the untested renormalization and the untested-weight-%.
  • scripts/fill-scorecard.mjs — emits the calibration anchor set per dimension plus a collapsed evidence-sweep block under each dimension's notes, and two root anchors.
  • SKILL.md — the Phase Score method rewrite, rubric materialization for descriptor-less sheets, the gate wired as a hard STOP, and Phase Coach given the method without the machinery.

Commits: 7c8003d3 (implementation), then bae2a016, 98b0b736, f18482d4, 184748f2, 0cc24618 and 3eed5081 — all six verification fixes, each closing a defect found after the implementation commit's own gates were green.

Phase 2 — static verification

CheckResult
node --check on all four scriptsclean
check-scoring-calibration.mjs --selftest31/31 PASS, exit 0
fill-scorecard.mjs --selftest57/57 PASS, exit 0
check-citations.mjs --selftest (INV-3)8/8 PASS, exit 0
tests/run-tests.shinterview-feedback tests: 10/10 PASS, exit 0

There is no repo-wide linter or type-checker for .mjs skill scripts in this repo; the four hygiene lints and skill-eval (below) are the equivalent gate.

Phase 3 — code quality review

One independent reviewer over git show 7c8003d3, covering all five angles (CLAUDE.md compliance, bug scan on changed lines, git-history context, comment compliance, cross-file anchor consistency).

#FindingConfidenceAction
1 A dimension that is neither scored nor tagged data-untested passes every gate while its weight leaves both the scored and the untested totals — silently shrinking the denominator. 78 Fixed in bae2a016, with a red-green regression fixture.
2 A sheet where some scored dimensions carry data-weight and others do not would zero out the unweighted ones rather than falling back to an unweighted mean (the fallback fires only when every weight is absent). 35 Noted, not blocking. /interview-guide's validate-scorecard-anchors.mjs already gates weights summing to 100 at authoring time, so a partially-weighted sheet cannot be emitted by the pipeline.
3 data-rubric-provenance is parsed but no gate asserts it, while SKILL.md calls the stamp "never skipped". 20 Noted, not blocking — and deliberate: AC3 enumerates exactly four gates and the stamp is not one of them. Candidate follow-up.

Angles yielding nothing: CLAUDE.md compliance, the string-manipulation helpers, the arithmetic (atBarLevel, modal tie-break, recoBand, all re-derived by hand), git-history context, comment compliance, and cross-file anchor consistency (zero drift across the three files).

Phase 4 — runtime verification

The change alters an emitted HTML artifact, so the browser sub-steps were run rather than classified away. The artifact was produced by the real CLI against the real skeleton — fill-scorecard.mjs fill ../_shared/interview-guidelines/scorecard-skeleton.html vals.json — served over http://localhost, and driven with Playwright.

Slop gate

Runner output, verbatim:

[slop-gate] 0 blocking quality fault(s), 0 quality / 4 slop finding(s) — no quality blocker; slop advisory only

Exit 0, ran: true. The four advisory slop tells (border-accent-on-rounded, overused-font, cream-palette, em-dash-overuse) all originate in scorecard-skeleton.html, which this story declares BYTE-FROZEN — none is introduced by this diff. Advisory only; never gating.

Browser walk (4d/4e)

AssertionObserved
Evidence-sweep block presentyes, one per scored dimension
Collapsed by default (D9)details.open === false
Positioned under that dimension's notes (D9)compareDocumentPosition → FOLLOWING
Every swept instance carries a citation2 instances, 2 with cite[data-cite-tier]
Root data-reco-rationale on the real element"At bar on both."
Root data-rubric-provenance on the real element"derived-from-jd; agreed 2026-07-21"
Artifact self-contained (no external asset)0 stylesheet/script/img references
Default reading view (scores + notes) unchangedscreenshot below

Interactive check: expanding one dimension's disclosure reveals its cited instance while the sibling stays collapsed — jb6-evidence-sweep-expanded.png in this directory.

End-to-end in both directions on the real skeleton:

$ node scripts/check-scoring-calibration.mjs filled.html
check-scoring-calibration: 2 dimensions, 0 failed
✓ calibration: weighted 3.00, modal 3, band yes, untested 0/2 (0.0% of weight)   [exit 0]

$ node scripts/check-scoring-calibration.mjs filled-bad.html
dimension "example-dimension-two" is scored (2) with no <details data-card="evidence-sweep"> block …
dimension "example-dimension-two" is scored 2, below its at-bar level 3, with no non-empty data-rebuttal …
check-scoring-calibration: 2 dimensions, 2 failed                                 [exit 1]

UX polish checklist (4f)

No wireframes exist for this story, so Part 1 does not apply. Part 2, on the emitted artifact:

#CheckOutcomeEvidence
P1document.title per routeNA — alt-evidenceTitle is {{round_name}} — {{candidate}} because the fixture fills a raw skeleton; those tokens are substituted by /interview-guide at authoring time, in the byte-frozen skeleton this story may not touch.
P2No internal IDs in user copyNA — alt-evidenceThe three snake_case strings found (round_name, lead_interviewer) are the same unsubstituted skeleton tokens; none is emitted by this diff.
P3Casing/format consistencyVerifiedThe one new visible string — the sweep summary — matches the artifact's sentence-case convention (screenshot).
P4Loading / empty / error statesVerifiedEmpty state exercised: a dimension with no sweep values renders no <details> at all rather than an empty shell (renderEvidenceSweep returns ''; selftest cal: calibration anchors are absent entirely when no calibration values are supplied). Error path is the gate's non-zero exit, shown above.
P5Meaningful alt textNA — alt-evidenceThe artifact contains zero images.
P6No dead disabled affordancesNA — alt-evidenceThe added surface is a native <details> disclosure; it has no disabled state.
P7/P8Hard-reload / deep-link parityVerifiedSingle-file artifact with no router; loaded fresh by URL, which is the only way it is ever opened.
P9Zero console errorsVerifiedClean on the post-fix load (the one error on the first load was the pre-fix artifact's favicon 404 from the throwaway static server, not the artifact).
P10Nav labels match destinationsNA — alt-evidenceNo navigation surface in the artifact.
P11Failure paths visibly recoverableVerifiedThe gate's failure message names the dimension and the missing anchor, and SKILL.md's non-zero-exit branch tells the operator what to repair before re-running.
P12No raw external/internal anchors leakedVerifiedquerySelectorAll('a') → zero non-app hrefs.

Defects found and fixed during verification

All seven were found after the implementation commit's own gates were green, and each carries red-green regression coverage. Two came from reviewers, one from the browser, and the last three from an independent judge that attacked the gate rather than read it — including one defect I introduced while fixing another.

1. The doc-comment was stamped instead of the element

fillCalibrationRoot matched the first <main data-card="scorecard"> in the file. The skeleton opens with a contract comment that documents the anchors by quoting that tag verbatim, so the attributes landed inside prose and the artifact shipped with no root anchors at all. The string assertion that "verified" it matched the same comment — a whole-file regex cannot tell an element from a description of one. Only the DOM query caught it (rootRecoRationale: null on a run whose selftest was green).

Fixed by skipping comment regions when locating the root, and by re-pointing the assertions at the comment-stripped body plus a new assertion that the comment is never stamped. check-scoring-calibration.mjs now strips comments before parsing for the same reason — a decoy <section data-dim> inside a comment was being parsed as a real, failing dimension.

Red-green: without the fix, 3 assertions fail (54/57); without the comment-strip, a clean two-dimension sheet reports 3 dimensions, 3 failed.

2. The forgotten dimension

A dimension that was neither scored nor tagged data-untested passed every gate — gates 1–3 fire only on a scored dimension — while its weight left both the scored and the untested totals, shrinking the denominator and inflating the weighted score. That is precisely the defect class this epic exists to close, and the gate did not catch it.

Now a hard failure, asserted ahead of the four gates as the arithmetic's precondition and documented in SKILL.md alongside them. Red-green: without it the fixture reports 2 dimensions, 0 failed and weighted 3.00, untested 0/2 on a sheet with an unresolved dimension — the failure mode, reproduced.

3. The citation-tier side door

Gate 1 required a citation on every swept instance, but checked only that a data-cite-tier existed. check-citations.mjs verifies only the transcript and submission tiers verbatim — notes and recalled are legal tiers there and exempt from the substring check by design. So a wholly fabricated scorecard whose sweep instances were tagged notes passed both blocking gates and emitted strong-yes: the exact anti-ritual property the epic exists to establish, defeated through the tier.

Found by an independent judge, which built and ran the exploit rather than reasoning about it. Fixed in 98b0b736 by restricting gate 1 to the tiers that are actually verified. Proven closed end-to-end in both directions: the exploit sheet now exits 1 on the calibration gate, and re-tagging it to transcript to get past that instead trips check-citations.mjs with "not a verbatim substring of the transcript". There is no longer a tier that is both accepted here and unverified there.

4. The fix took the notes-only path offline

Restricting the tiers unconditionally broke a documented, honest case. Phase Ground defines three grounding tiers, and a round with no recording and no transcript — graded from the interviewer's own written notes — is explicitly an unattended-safe path. Such a round can never produce a transcript-tier citation, so gate 1 became unpassable for it, for reasons unrelated to whether it was scored honestly. The same judge caught this on the re-judge, on the fix it had itself asked for.

Fixed in f18482d4 by deciding the tier set from the filesystem rather than the sheet: a transcript beside the scorecard means the strict set, its absence means notes also grounds an instance, announced on stdout every time the downgrade applies. A fabricated sheet in a transcript round therefore still cannot relabel its way into the weaker set, so defect 3 stays closed; recalled is accepted at neither setting. checkCalibration defaults to the strict reading, so a caller that forgets to probe fails safe.

Red-green as a pair on one byte-identical sheet through the real CLI — FAIL-g1-unverifiable-tier (transcript present) against PASS-g1-notes-tier-when-no-transcript (absent) — plus FAIL-g1-recalled-tier-even-without-transcript, so what landed is a carve-out for notes and not a hole for any tier. Either half alone would have proved nothing.

5. The carve-out could be bought with one cp

Deciding the tier set from the filesystem made it a function of where the gate ran. The judge copied a fabricated notes-tier scorecard out of its round folder into an empty directory and re-ran the identical command: the sheet that correctly failed in place passed clean with strong-yes. "No transcript beside this path" is a fact about the invocation, not about the round — defect 3's hole, reopened through a path door instead of a tier door, by the fix for defect 4.

Fixed in 184748f2 by inverting what the filesystem is allowed to do. The downgrade is now declared (--no-transcript) and corroborated: the flag is refused with exit 2 when a transcript does sit beside the scorecard. The filesystem can no longer grant the relaxation, only veto a false claim to it — which kills the honest-mistake case (a caller passing the flag by habit in a real transcript round fails loudly) without pretending a flag is unforgeable. A relaxation nobody has to ask for is one nobody can see; this one is on the command line and in the output every time it applies.

Red-green as a four-cell matrix over one byte-identical sheet, plus the judge's own exploit re-run end-to-end: in place → fail · copied, identical command → fail (was: pass) · copied and declared → pass · declared in place → exit 2.

6. The notes-only path crashed on the sibling gate

SKILL.md claimed tier-2 was a working path while its own instruction ran check-citations.mjs filled-scorecard.html transcript.refined.txt — whose second positional is an unguarded readFileSync. In a genuine notes-only round that file does not exist, so the documented instruction dies on an uncaught ENOENT. Worse, the obvious workaround is a trap: notes-tier citations never read the transcript argument's contents, so substituting any file yields a green ✓ citations: line — a pass bought with a dummy, the exact ritual shape these gates exist to refuse.

check-citations.mjs is byte-frozen (INV-3), so the instruction is what changed. Tier-2 rounds now run the calibration gate with --no-transcript instead of the citation gate, SKILL.md explicitly forbids the placeholder workaround and says why, and the calibration gate absorbs the two checks the citation gate would still have been making: no transcript- or submission-tier citation may appear in a transcript-less round (a lie by construction), and every <cite> must name a non-empty data-source. Both carry fail fixtures.

7. The veto only knew two filenames in one directory

The corroboration in defect 5 was an exact-match allowlist — transcript.refined.txt and transcript.whisper.txt, in the scorecard's own directory. Two attacks got past it with the scorecard never moving, which is what makes them worse than the cp hole: a real transcript named transcript.txt sitting right beside the sheet, and a real transcript one directory up with the sheet in a nested/ outputs folder. Both are plausible-looking layouts, and "did you run this from inside the round folder?" cannot catch either.

Fixed in 0cc24618 by making the veto greedy. The asymmetry is the design argument, and it is recorded in the constant's comment: a false veto is loud, names the offending file, and costs the operator seconds; a missed transcript is silent. Both of the judge's fixtures then exited 2.

The judge's final pass found two narrower variants still open — a prefixed name (raw_transcript.txt, the shape real tools emit) and a transcript two levels up — and recorded them as non-blocking, suggesting the boundary simply be documented. Both were cheaper to close than to write down, so 3eed5081 takes the same asymmetry argument all the way: contains-match rather than prefix-anchored, three levels rather than one. Verified on the judge's combined shape (prefixed name, two levels up, sheet unmoved) → exit 2.

8. A written submission was treated as a lie

The transcript-less mode flagged submission-tier citations as impossible-by-construction. That is wrong: a take-home's existence has nothing to do with whether the live round was recorded, so a notes-graded round can hold a genuine, verifiable submission quote. Worse, such a round had no passing path through either gate, since the citation gate takes a transcript as a required positional.

Only the transcript tier is now a lie under the flag, and SKILL.md routes the submission-bearing round explicitly: pass the submission file in both positional slots of check-citations.mjs. This is deliberately distinguished from the placeholder trick the same section forbids — every check that runs is a real one. The second slot goes unread precisely because the calibration gate has already made a transcript-tier citation impossible on this path; the third is what verifies the submission quotes verbatim.

Phase 5a — story acceptance criteria

ACRequirementOutcomeEvidence
AC1FR-4 — Phase Score reads data-level; descriptor-less sheets route to AC5, never to uncalibrated scoring (INV-1)VerifiedSKILL.md clause (b) reads the descriptors and routes descriptor-less sheets to #rubric-materialization; the degrade path is explicitly named retired.
AC2FR-5 — the four-clause method rewrite (a) sweep-then-score (b) quote the bar (c) floor/ceiling (d) untestedVerifiedAll four clauses present under #scoring-method, in that order; three of the four are machine-enforced by the gate.
AC3FR-6 — second blocking gate enforcing sweep, adversarial rebuttal, note-vs-score, reco-vs-modalVerifiedAll four implemented and each covered by a pass and a fail fixture; wired into Phase Score as a hard STOP alongside (not replacing) the citation gate; non-zero exit demonstrated end-to-end above.
AC4FR-6/D7 — untested excluded from the denominator, weights renormalize, untested-% reported, 30% threshold one named constant, no model arithmeticVerifiedUNTESTED_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD_PCT = 30 is the single constant; the renormalization worked example asserts the literal output line weighted 4.00, modal 4, band strong-yes, untested 1/3 (60.0% of weight); the 29%/30% boundary pair is a fixture; SKILL.md carries no instruction to compute anything (grep for compute/average/weight/total returns only the prohibition).
AC5FR-7 — rubric materialization: derive, write into the sheet, agree (blocking), stamp provenanceVerified#rubric-materialization steps 1–4; the AskUserQuestion carries a (Recommended) option and is deliberately not defer-only-tagged so a headless run AUTO-PICKs; all three provenance tokens (authored / synthesized-agreed / synthesized-auto) named, stamped unconditionally.
AC6FR-6/D9 — fill-scorecard.mjs emits the anchors; artifact self-contained; default reading experience unchangedVerified13 new selftest assertions plus the browser table above: collapsed, under the notes, zero external assets, and anchors entirely absent when no calibration values are supplied.
AC7FR-8/D11 — Phase Coach gets the method, no gate against output (b), no new anchors on itVerifiedPhase Coach carries sweep-then-conclude and the adversarial pass as method, and states why the gate structurally cannot run against (b); interviewer-notes-skeleton.html is byte-frozen (empty diff).
AC8Selftests per gate; citation gate untouched (INV-3); no new flag, argument-hint unchanged, NI block byte-identical (INV-7); skill-eval and four lints greenVerified31/31 + 57/57 + 8/8 + 10/10; git diff empty for check-citations.mjs, all of skills/interview-guide/ and all of _shared/interview-guidelines/; frontmatter untouched; lint-non-interactive-inline.sh PASS: all 61 supported skills match canonical; skill-eval 20/20 exit 0.

Phase 5c — plan task compliance

TaskOutcomeEvidence
T1 studyVerified-completeInsertion points pinned; the absence of any weighted/modal routine confirmed in code, which is what made the gate a new capability rather than a re-homing.
T2 emit the anchor setVerified-complete7c8003d3 + bae2a016; 13 cal: selftest assertions; browser table.
T3 method rewriteVerified-complete7c8003d3; AC2 row.
T4 sweep / adversarial / note-vs-score gatesVerified-complete7c8003d3; pass+fail fixture per gate; at-bar level read from the sheet (the 1–6 scale fixture proves it is not hardcoded).
T5 the arithmeticVerified-complete7c8003d3; renormalization worked example and the 29/30 boundary pair.
T6 wire as second blocking gateVerified-complete7c8003d3; end-to-end non-zero exit demonstrated.
T7 rubric materializationVerified-complete7c8003d3; AC5 row; audit-recommended.sh confirms the call is marked.
T8 Coach gets the methodVerified-complete7c8003d3; AC7 row.
T9 conformanceVerified-completeAll eight sub-items executed. The anti-ritual re-read (item 2) changed the product: gate 1 was strengthened mid-task to require a citation on every swept instance, because a timestamp alone can be invented.

Phase 7 — hard gates

GateResult
skill-eval-check.sh (the [D] half, re-run per the route:skill bridge)21 checks, all pass, 0 residuals, exit 0
lint-flags-vs-hints.shPASS
lint-phase-refs.shPASS — every phase reference resolves
lint-non-interactive-inline.shPASS — all 61 skills match canonical
audit-recommended.shPASS — 3 calls, 2 Recommended, 1 defer-only, 0 unmarked
check-comments-coverage.shPASS
check-browser-evidence.shOK — trigger did not fire on the changed-file patterns; the browser walk was run anyway

Dogfood verdict

satisfied — overall_satisfied: true.

tasks.yaml carries no TN−1 dogfood task and the story is type: enhancement (T2), so the gate reads strictly as "missing → PASS-WITH-GAPS". Rather than record a verdict line nobody earned, an independent judge was dispatched to attack the gate — the one thing this story cannot verify about itself, since a gate written and graded by the same agent proves only self-consistency.

That was the highest-value decision of the build. The judge ran five passes and produced four of the seven defects, including the two most serious. Its own summary of what it threw at the story: "everything I threw at this across four adversarial passes that constituted a real, working, low-effort fabrication-and-pass exploit has been fixed and I independently re-verified each fix with fresh fixtures (not just the shipped selftest)" — the notes-tier side door, the cp-to-empty-dir relocation, two filename/directory evasions, the check-citations.mjs ENOENT crash, and the submission-tier false positive. It also verified the arithmetic by hand and confirmed the both-slots submission invocation genuinely rejects a fabricated submission quote rather than rubber-stamping it.

One of its findings it then withdrew under argument (the lexical-overlap floor against quote laundering), which is recorded as considered-and-rejected rather than silently dropped.

Open items

Three named residuals, all disclosed in SKILL.md beside the gate rather than left implicit. None blocks the story; each is a limit of what a script can honestly prove.

  1. Completeness — the gate proves each swept instance is real, not that the sweep found every instance. No presence check can distinguish one representative instance from the only one you looked at; that half of clause (a) rests on the method. A timestamp-span proxy was proposed and deferred as a future iteration, not adopted.
  2. Relevance — a genuine verbatim span can sit under invented interpretive prose. A lexical-overlap floor was considered and rejected: good notes paraphrase, so it would fail honest analytical prose while passing keyword-padded fabrication, and it would recreate the rewrite-to-match pressure gate 3 exists to refuse. The judge accepted this and withdrew the finding.
  3. The veto reads where it is pointed — the tier-2 declaration is corroborated eagerly (any filename containing transcript, three levels up), so a false declaration fails loudly on the documented layout and every near miss tested. A caller who puts the sheet where no transcript is reachable and declares the downgrade still gets through. No gate reading only the files it is handed can beat a caller who chooses what to hand it.

Two non-blocking review notes carried forward as candidate follow-ups: the partially-weighted-sheet fallback (confidence 35, unreachable through the authoring pipeline, which gates weights to sum to 100) and the unasserted data-rubric-provenance stamp (confidence 20 — deliberate, since AC3 enumerates exactly four gates and the stamp is not one of them).

pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-07-21_interview-scoring-calibration/stories/260721-jb6/verify/2026-07-21-review.html · Created using pmos‑toolkit
